pr.title: Split up more stage files in text_2 and down some into text_3
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large assembly-only organization PR that carved multiple stage-related regions out of the monolithic asm/text_2.s and asm/text_3.s blobs into named asm/gr*.s files, then updated ldscript.lcf so the new object boundaries preserved link order. The PR body says the split went upward to the grbigbluecar.o region, stopping because the author was not yet sure how it crosses into grzakogenerator.o, and downward to the start of grpura.o / Poke Floats. There was no recorded review discussion in the provided slice.
what_changed: Removed 14169 lines from asm/text_2.s and 10439 lines from asm/text_3.s, replacing monolithic text blobs with separately named stage assembly files.

Added new stage assembly files: asm/grstadium.s, asm/grcastle.s, asm/grkongo.s, asm/grshrineroute.s, asm/grbigblueroute.s, asm/grizumi.s, asm/groldpupupu.s, asm/groldkongo.s, asm/groldyoshi.s, asm/grzebesroute.s, asm/grfigure1.s, asm/grfigure2.s, and asm/grfigure3.s.
Updated ldscript.lcf with 31 added lines across 4 hunks so the newly split assembly objects are linked in the intended address/order position.

The diff excerpt shows the new files retain raw assembly structure with macros.inc inclusion, .section .text, .global declarations, address comments, labels such as lbl_8020B884, and function symbols such as func_8020B864 and func_8020B9D4.

The PR body explicitly documents boundary uncertainty: the author split up to the grbigbluecar.o region but stopped because they were not sure where it goes into grzakogenerator.o, and split down to the start of grpura.o / Poke Floats.
smart_moves: Split only across boundaries the author felt confident about, explicitly leaving the uncertain grbigbluecar.o-to-grzakogenerator.o area unresolved rather than guessing object ownership.
Preserved linkability by pairing the asm file split with ldscript.lcf changes instead of only moving text between files.
Used stage/object-style filenames with gr prefixes, making future decomp work easier to search and assign by stage subsystem instead of by giant text_2/text_3 offsets.
Kept original assembly labels, globals, section declarations, and address comments intact, which reduces risk during mechanical splitting and supports future function-by-function decompilation.
decomp_lessons: When breaking up monolithic asm/text_N.s files, treat object boundary identification as a first-class task; do not force a split through an uncertain transition just to make the file layout look complete.
Every assembly split that creates new object files must be reflected in the linker script in the same order as the original binary layout, or matching/linking can silently drift.
Large mechanical splits are useful even before C decompilation because they create stable, searchable units for later symbol naming, stage ownership analysis, and incremental matching.
Document uncertain regions in the PR body; future contributors can then target follow-up work around named boundaries like grbigbluecar.o, grzakogenerator.o, and grpura.o.
Preserving raw labels and address comments during file extraction makes later verification and bisecting easier because moved code can still be correlated with original ROM addresses.
naming_conventions: Stage assembly files use lowercase gr-prefixed names, e.g. grstadium.s, grcastle.s, grkongo.s, grshrineroute.s, grbigblueroute.s, grizumi.s, groldpupupu.s, groldkongo.s, groldyoshi.s, grzebesroute.s, grfigure1.s, grfigure2.s, and grfigure3.s.
Unidentified functions remain in func_80XXXXXX form, e.g. func_8020B864 and func_8020B9D4.
Unidentified local labels remain in lbl_80XXXXXX form, e.g. lbl_8020B884, lbl_8020B94C, and lbl_8020BA98.
Small-data and global labels retain generated lbl_804DXXXX / lbl_8049XXXX style names when semantics are not known.
Object boundary names mentioned in the PR body follow stage object naming with .o suffixes, e.g. grbigbluecar.o, grzakogenerator.o, and grpura.o.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Mechanical extraction from asm/text_2.s and asm/text_3.s into per-stage asm files while preserving instruction text and labels.
New asm files include macros.inc and declare .section .text to match existing assembly file format.
Link order is controlled through ldscript.lcf updates after adding new asm files.
Boundary selection was conservative: split to known stage regions and stopped before an uncertain object transition.
Retained address comments beside instructions, enabling later checks that moved code still corresponds to the original address range.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
No reviewer-requested naming, struct, or matching changes can be inferred from the available evidence.
follow_up_queries: Find the later PR that splits or identifies the uncertain grbigbluecar.o to grzakogenerator.o boundary.
Find follow-up PRs that decompile any of the new stage asm files added here, especially grstadium, grcastle, grkongo, and grbigblueroute.
Inspect subsequent ldscript.lcf changes around grpura.o / Poke Floats to see how the next stage boundary was handled.
Search for later naming of functions in the func_8020B864 through func_8020DE48 range.
